title: Update .rules for canonical transaction ordering, task lifecycle prerequisites, and commit gating expectations
summary: Rules alignment is complete. `.rules` now documents a strict canonical-flow-only transaction model in which tx.begin precedes task lifecycle work, tx.verify.start precedes verify results, tx.verify.pass precedes verified file-intent transitions, file-intent updates require prior registration, and commit operations depend on a valid verification sequence and existing transaction context.
acceptance_criteria: .rules explicitly documents that tx.begin must occur before task lifecycle events such as task start/update/end.
.rules explicitly documents that tx.verify.start must occur before tx.verify.pass or tx.verify.fail.
.rules explicitly documents that tx.verify.pass must occur before tx.file_intent.update can set state=verified.
.rules explicitly documents that file intent updates require a previously registered file intent for the same path.
.rules explicitly documents commit gating expectations so commit operations only occur after a valid verify sequence.
The documented ordering and commit expectations are consistent with the implementation and generated planning artifacts.

steps.description: Review current .rules wording for task lifecycle flow, verification ordering, file-intent transitions, and commit behavior.
steps.progress: Reviewed the updated `.rules` wording alongside the 0.4.5 plan and Phase 2 implementation outcomes. Confirmed the key alignment targets are explicit tx.begin prerequisites for task lifecycle work, verify.start before verify results, verify.pass before verified file-intent transitions, and commit gating on the canonical verify sequence.

steps.description: Update .rules so canonical ordering requirements are explicit for tx.begin, verify events, and file-intent verified transitions.
steps.progress: Updated `.rules` so canonical ordering requirements are explicit: tx.begin must occur before task lifecycle events, tx.verify.start must occur before tx.verify.pass/tx.verify.fail, tx.verify.pass must occur before tx.file_intent.update can set state=verified, and file-intent updates require a previously registered intent for the same path.

steps.description: Clarify .rules commit gating expectations so commit operations depend on a valid verification sequence and existing transaction context.
steps.progress: Updated `.rules` to state that commit operations require a valid verify sequence and existing transaction context, matching repository-local enforcement that rejects commit attempts which bypass canonical verification ordering.

steps.description: Cross-check the updated .rules wording against the 0.4.5 plan and ticket set to keep planning artifacts and implementation expectations aligned.
steps.progress: Cross-checked the revised `.rules` wording against the 0.4.5 plan, ticket set, and Phase 2 implementation behavior. The final rules text now consistently treats the canonical transaction flow as the only documented operating model, without preserving helper-convenience exceptions in the rules.

notes: Keep the rules strict; this ticket is about alignment and clarity, not relaxing ordering guarantees.
Prefer explicit sequencing language to avoid ambiguous operational guidance.
Make commit preconditions clear enough to prevent invalid commit attempts after incomplete or synthetic verification flows.
